text:
Elena Rybakinahas become the latest big-name player to withdraw from theOlympicsdespite being included in Thursday's draw. The 25-year-old was due to represent Kazakhstan in the women's singles event but it has been confirmed that she will play no part in the Games.

Rybakina, who came fourth in Tokyo to narrowly miss out on an Olympic medal, was also expected to play in the mixed doubles alongside Alexander Bublik. However, it was revealed just hours after the draw was made that she had withdrawn from both events.

The one-time Grand Slam winner was due to face Jacqueline Cristan in the first round, with Caroline Garcia moving into her seeded spot. Daria Saville has been called up as an alternate to replace Garcia, who was originally drawn against Qinwen Zheng.

In the mixed doubles, Rybakina and Bublik have been replaced by Argentine duo Nadia Podoroska and Maximo Gonzalez, who will face the American pairing ofCoco Gauffand Taylor Fritz.

Rybakina is one of several high-profile players who will be absent from the Olympics following her late withdrawal. The likes ofAryna Sabalenka, Ons Jabeur, Marketa Vondrousova and Madison Keys will also not be playing in the women's singles event at Roland Garros.

A press release from the Kazakh tennis federation said Rybakina was suffering from 'acute bronchitis' and was advised by doctors not to travel to Paris.She has openly struggled with illness for much of this yearand withdrew from the Dubai Open, Indian Wells and the Italian Open as well as the Eastbourne International in the run-up toWimbledon.

She opened up on her recent health issues during her SW19 campaign, saying: "It's not easy because sometimes you have more energy and you want to do some interesting stuff. Sometimes you are too tired and you just want to be on the couch after the matches.

Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

"It's just not easy to balance it out and your wishes and also be prepared for the next day. So, I mean it's just you're trying to balance it out, really."

Rybakina's withdrawal from the Olympics came afterAndy Murrayconfirmed that he would not be playing in the men's singles event. He will take part in the doubles competition alongsideDan Evans, with the pair facing Japan duo Kei Nishikori and Taro Daniel in the first round.

Jannik Sinnerhas also withdrawn from the Games with tonsillitis after struggling with illness during hisWimbledonquarter-final defeat toDaniil Medvedev. He was among the favourites for the gold medal after winning his first Grand Slam title at theAustralian Openin January.

title: Tennis star pulls out of Olympics just hours after draw was made | Tennis | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Archie Griggs
description: Elena Rybakina has withdrawn from the Olympics despite being included in Thursday's draw.
